title: Attested Signing Ceremony: a TPM-2.0 reference implementation for measured-signing evidence
description: Reference implementation of the Attested Signing Ceremony described in §6.8.1 of Ficción Jurídica de la Auditoría Delegada. It binds each signed transaction payload to a hardware TPM 2.0 attestation (“measured signing”), commits the attestation digest to a SCITT-style append-only transparency log, and anchors it on-chain via a Bitcoin OP_RETURN output. It demonstrates that a presentation-layer attack — the pattern behind the Bybit, Radiant Capital and WazirX heists — becomes forensically irrefutable: the attestation records the payload that was actually signed, independently of what a compromised interface displayed. All TPM operations run against a real hardware TPM via the in-kernel resource manager.
